title: San Francisco · Forty Years of Crime
subtitle: FBI UCR 1985–2002 + SFPD incidents 2003–2026 · 41 neighborhoods · real locations

hook.sub: SFPD via DataSF · every neighborhood, every month

copy.chapter2Kicker: CHAPTER 2 · THE LAST 5 YEARS · SFPD / DATASF
copy.chapter2Caption: Monthly reports, 2021–2026. Every dot is a real reported incident location (block-level, sampled).
copy.transitionDesc: The chart counted per year. From here the map counts per month across 41 neighborhoods — every dot a real reported location, anonymized to the block.
copy.methodRecentSub: the last five years · month by month · real locations
copy.methodDotsSub: Every dot is a real reported incident location (block/intersection level). 98.1% map to a neighborhood — the rest counted, disclosed, never invented.
copy.methodFootnote: FBI UCR (through 2002) and SFPD incident data (2003+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, not one line.
copy.quizQuestion: Which San Francisco neighborhood is the safest?
copy.seamExplain: The ruler changes here — not the city. Through 2002 the gray bars are the FBI's yearly tally of San Francisco's classic “index” crimes — murder, robbery, assault, burglary, theft. From 2003 the figures are every incident SFPD records — a far broader net than the FBI's eight index crimes — so the level steps up because the measure widened, not because crime jumped. Compare the shape within one color, never across the dashed line.
copy.sourceLine: Data: San Francisco Police Department via DataSF (public domain) · block-level locations
copy.coverageText: 98.1% of records mapped to a neighborhood
copy.creditsSources: Sources: SFPD incident reports (2003–2018 & 2018–present, ODC-PDDL public domain) via DataSF & FBI UCR (1985–2002) via the FBI Crime Data Explorer. Neighborhoods: DataSF Analysis Neighborhoods; 2003–2017 incidents assigned by their real coordinates. Basemap: © OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL).

historyNotes.text: 1992: the peak — 77,015 crimes in one year.
The late-'90s fall is steep and steady.
2002: 42,898 — down 44% from the peak; the incident record begins.
2020: lockdown craters reports — April falls 34% below January.

annotations.text: Every dot is a real reported incident location — SFPD via DataSF, block and intersection level.
July 2023 — the busiest month of the last five years: 11,619 mapped reports.
The Tenderloin — San Francisco's busiest area for reported crime, nearly flat while the city fell.
North Beach: down 68% since 2022 — the city's steepest drop.
